## Summary

I extracted **5 program graphs** from the Osaka fork `exceptions.py` specification:

**Files created** (all under `/home/gohan/ga-runners/actions-runner-security-agent-2/_work/security-agent/security-agent/outputs/graphs/W4B5_1770278556/osaka-exceptions/`):

| ID | File | Description |
|:---|:-----|:------------|
| SG-001 | `SG-001_TransactionTypeError_init.mmd` | Constructor for `TransactionTypeError` -- stores the unknown transaction type byte and formats the error message |
| SG-002 | `SG-002_TransactionTypeContractCreationError_init.mmd` | Constructor for `TransactionTypeContractCreationError` -- stores the offending transaction and formats error with the type name |
| SG-003 | `SG-003_InsufficientMaxFeePerGasError_init.mmd` | Constructor for `InsufficientMaxFeePerGasError` -- stores both fee values (`transaction_max_fee_per_gas` and `block_base_fee_per_gas`) as `Final[Uint]` |
| SG-004 | `SG-004_transaction_validation_dispatch.mmd` | Complete transaction validation dispatch flow showing how all 12 exception types form a decision tree from general checks (type, contract creation, fees) to specific checks (blob data, authorization lists) |
| SG-005 | `SG-005_blob_transaction_validation.mmd` | Focused blob transaction validation sub-flow covering data presence, count limits, gas limits, fee sufficiency, and versioned hash verification with a loop |

The three constructor graphs (SG-001 through SG-003) capture the only classes with explicit `__init__` logic. The remaining 9 exception classes (`BlobGasLimitExceededError`, `InsufficientMaxFeePerBlobGasError`, `InvalidBlobVersionedHashError`, `NoBlobDataError`, `BlobCountExceededError`, `PriorityFeeGreaterThanMaxFeeError`, `EmptyAuthorizationListError`, `InitCodeTooLargeError`, `TransactionGasLimitExceededError`) are marker exceptions with no constructor logic -- they inherit directly from `InvalidTransaction` and are captured in SG-004 and SG-005 as the error-raising actions within the validation flows.
